pr.title: Split out rest of common items
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Merged one-commit PR by r-burns titled "Split out rest of common items." Available metadata indicates a large item-system code organization change: 43 changed files with 30,848 additions and 29,769 deletions. The PR body, comments, review comments, and usable diff/file list were absent from the slice; the GitHub diff fetch failed because the diff exceeded the API line limit. Evidence supports classifying this as a broad split-out/refactor of remaining common item code, but exact symbols, file paths, and matching techniques are not recoverable from the provided slice.
what_changed: Split out the remaining common item code, according to the PR title.
GitHub PR metadata reports 43 changed files, 30,848 additions, and 29,769 deletions, suggesting a large reorganization rather than a small isolated implementation.
The PR had one commit and was merged shortly after creation with no recorded issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the provided slice.
smart_moves: Completed a broad common-item split as a cohesive PR/branch, which likely made the item source layout more modular and searchable.
Kept the PR focused on common items rather than mixing in unrelated systems, based on the title and branch name "items".
Because the available evidence lacks the diff, no specific clever matching trick, symbol-level cleanup, or reviewer-approved tactic can be attributed safely.
decomp_lessons: Large split-out PRs can be important decomp infrastructure work even when they do not expose new behavior: separating common item code improves future per-item matching, reviewability, and navigation.

For archival and future-agent use, large PRs need changed-file metadata captured separately from the unified diff; this slice lost exact file evidence because the diff exceeded GitHub's 20,000-line API limit.
When only title-level evidence is available, avoid inferring exact functions or item names; record the architectural intent and mark confidence accordingly.
naming_conventions: The phrase "common items" is the only supported naming signal from the PR title.
No exact file, function, struct, or symbol naming conventions are visible in the provided PR slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactics are evidenced in the available title/body/comments/diff slice.
The additions/deletions scale suggests movement or splitting of code may have been involved, but exact matching tactics cannot be verified.
